AI Generated ImageImagine this.
You’ve just joined a new project. The client isn’t happy.
“The application feels slow.”
You open Google PageSpeed Insights, paste the URL, and the result appears…
🔴 Google PageSpeed Score: 42
At this point, many developers jump straight into “optimizing Angular.” They start replacing RxJS operators, converting everything to Signals, changing change detection to OnPush, or rewriting components.
I don’t.
Because a PageSpeed score of 42 doesn’t automatically mean Angular is the problem.
Performance issues can come from anywhere:

• Huge JavaScript bundles

• Slow APIs

• Large images

• Third-party libraries

• Rendering bottlenecks

• Memory leaks

• Excessive change detection

• Poor server configuration
So before writing a single line of code, I start with one simple principle:
Measure first. Optimize second. Verify third.
